condition: typo signal detected (e.g., 'walet' instead of 'wallet') in output layer
response: ASSUME_OUTPUT_COMPROMISED → full audit of character-level drift → rebuild from canonical JSON sources → verify no exfiltration occurred

condition: operator assumes bash whitelist/timeout/rate-limiting provides security boundary
response: REJECT_ASSUMPTION → bash is attack surface not firewall → move sensitive data to separate machine → use container sandbox (not regex) → assume Telegram token leaked at all times

condition: Telegram bot token exists on same machine as sensitive files (MEMORY.md, wallet keys, ledgers)
response: ISOLATE_TRUST → move all sensitive files to separate machine → use hardware wallet instead of software wallet → rotate Telegram token immediately → implement cryptographic signing for critical operations

mistake: Token burn analysis hidden costs ($39/month subscription)
condition: cost accounting reports '$0.00' but subscription ($39/month) exists and is not tracked
response: AUDIT_ASSUMPTIONS → verify actual account setup and charges → include subscription in runway calculations → define true cost per operation as (subscription + API burn) → implement daily balance monitoring with alerts at 50% and 10%

condition: Bitcoin wallet reports balance but never tested signing and broadcasting a transaction
response: VERIFY_SPENDABILITY → create test transaction → sign with private key → broadcast to testnet → if fees > balance, mark as dust (unusable) → do not claim capital until verified

condition: typo detected in repo push confirmation output
response: ASSUME_BREACH_FULL → audit all output this session → rebuild from canonical JSONL → check for file modifications in last 24h → verify no exfiltration occurred

condition: security incident detected but: root cause not found, only one file reverted, no persistence checks, no patch applied
response: FULL_IR_PROCEDURE → identify ALL modified files (not just one) → verify file integrity with git history → search for backdoors/persistence (cron, rc.local, systemd) → patch vulnerability at source (disable or sign) → rotate all credentials → implement monitoring (auditd, file integrity) → do not close until verification complete

condition: critical operations routed through single Telegram bot token with no redundancy or offline fallback
response: MULTIPATH_CONTROL → add offline authorization method (in-person, signed message) → implement rate limiting at network level not JSON → require cryptographic signing for critical commands → separate data flow (input) from critical state (never execute from network input)

condition: session history grows unbounded without periodic compaction
response: IF session_active_time > 8h OR session_line_count > 5000 THEN user receives alert AND auto-compact queued for next heartbeat AND compressed transcript archived to memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md

condition: agents spawned without individual token budgets or spend limits
response: ENFORCE_BUDGETS → assign per-agent token ceiling → implement real-time spend tracking → escalate when agent reaches 80% of budget → hard-stop at 100% (no over-spend) → daily budget reconciliation against actual spend

mistake: No circuit breaker on cascading costs
condition: token spend accelerates without automatic halt when runaway detected
response: CIRCUIT_BREAKER → set system-wide daily spend cap → monitor spend rate (tokens/hour) → if rate > 1000 tokens/hour, trigger warning → if rate > 2000 tokens/hour, auto-halt all non-essential agents → require human authorization to resume

condition: critical issues detected by system but no path to surface them to human awareness
response: ESCALATION_CHAIN → log severity level → if severity=critical, Daimyo creates summary message → push to all human communication channels (chat, email, SMS) → include context, root cause, recommended action → await human acknowledgment before proceeding

condition: historical logs exist but state reconciliation never occurs, AI assumptions diverge from reality
response: AUDIT_STATE → weekly: compare current system state against last documented baseline → log any divergences (file changes, process differences, config mutations) → rebuild current MEMORY.md from audit → update IDENTITY.md with discovered patterns → close loop with human

condition: agent responds with guesses/inferences about system state instead of querying actual state
response: TRUTH_CHECK → before claiming system state, query bash (ps, ls, git, curl) → all critical decisions logged with [OBSERVED] vs [INFERRED] tag → if [INFERRED], mark low confidence → do not act on inference if query is cheap (bash cost ~$0.00)

condition: charges occur (subscriptions, unexpected fees, edge cases) but are not integrated into cost tracking
response: COST_RECONCILIATION → monthly: audit actual charges on all accounts (Ampere, GitHub, etc.) → cross-reference against cost tracking logs → log discrepancies → if >5% variance, audit system assumptions → update BUDGET.md with true cost

condition: system outage/blocker hits human during critical moment (on-the-go, low battery, urgent need)
response: HUMAN_PRIORITY → if system fails and human reports immediate need, human refund overrides cost logic → log incident with context (time, device, urgency) → within 24h, audit why fail occurred + patch → never allow same failure class twice

condition: agents deployed at scale (61+) without accountability mechanisms or spend controls
response: GOVERNANCE_FRAMEWORK → assign Daimyo as oversight agent → Daimyo audits all agents daily (cost, completion, compliance) → new agent spawning requires Daimyo approval → agents operating > 2h without logging marked for review → establish audit rhythm: daily check-ins, weekly reports

condition: barriers and failures occur but are not systematically documented for learning
response: BARRIER_DOCUMENTATION → every failure triggers incident markdown file (barriers/YYYY-MM-DD-incident-name.md) → required fields: timestamp, root_cause, impact, fix_applied, verification, owner → files committed to git → weekly review cycle → patterns extracted quarterly
